description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished an experiment and need to archive its artifacts for reproducibility checks.

Pre-existing setup in the container  
------------------------------------------------  
1. Directory with artifacts:  
   • /home/user/experiments/run_01/metrics.json  
   • /home/user/experiments/run_01/model.bin  
   • /home/user/experiments/run_01/logs.txt  

2. An (empty) directory to hold archives already exists:  
   • /home/user/archives  

Goal  
----  
1. Create a compressed archive (tar + gzip) that contains *exactly* the three files inside /home/user/experiments/run_01.  
   • Name: run_01_artifacts.tar.gz  
   • Location: /home/user/archives/run_01_artifacts.tar.gz  

2. Extract the archive into the following path (which does **not** exist yet; create it):  
   • /home/user/restore/run_01/  
   After extraction the three files must appear directly under /home/user/restore/run_01 (no extra leading directory level).

3. Produce a plain-text log file at /home/user/artifact_checksums.log recording SHA-256 checksums for the archive and the three extracted files.  
   • Exactly four lines, **newline-terminated** (write the trailing newline after the last line).  
   • Each line must follow this *exact* template (two spaces between hash and path):  
        SHA256_SUM  ABSOLUTE_PATH  
     Example:  
        e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  /home/user/archives/run_01_artifacts.tar.gz  

   • Line order MUST be:  
        1) the archive (/home/user/archives/run_01_artifacts.tar.gz)  
        2) /home/user/restore/run_01/metrics.json  
        3) /home/user/restore/run_01/model.bin  
        4) /home/user/restore/run_01/logs.txt  

What the automated checker will verify  
--------------------------------------  
• Archive file exists at the exact path and contains only the three required files with correct relative paths.  
• All three files are present in /home/user/restore/run_01/ and their contents are byte-identical to the originals.  
• The log file exists, has exactly four lines in the specified order, each line formatted as  
      64-hex-char-hash␣␣/absolute/path\n  
  (two spaces, ending newline).  
• Hashes in the log must match actual sha256sum results of the files.  

You may use any standard GNU/Linux command-line utilities available in a typical distribution. No sudo access is available. The entire task can be achieved with a small sequence of commands (≈2-3 main commands plus any commands you need for checksum calculation).
truth:
Initial files and their exact contents:

1. /home/user/experiments/run_01/metrics.json  
   ------------------------------------------------
   {
     "accuracy": 0.94,
     "loss": 0.23
   }
   (newline at end)

2. /home/user/experiments/run_01/model.bin  
   ------------------------------------------------
   MODEL_WEIGHTS
   (newline at end)

3. /home/user/experiments/run_01/logs.txt  
   ------------------------------------------------
   Training started...
   Training completed successfully.
   (newline at end)

Expected artefacts after successful completion:

A) /home/user/archives/run_01_artifacts.tar.gz  
   It must be a gzip-compressed tarball whose tar index lists exactly:
     metrics.json
     model.bin
     logs.txt
   with no leading directory component. When extracted, each file’s contents must be byte-identical to the originals above.

B) Directory /home/user/restore/run_01/ containing byte-identical copies of the three files.

C) /home/user/artifact_checksums.log  
   Four newline-terminated lines. Example layout (*hash values will differ*):
     de9b2c7ee0e4eaceb6ed6a43a65242cba2ff3c62495bf7755bac109e61b8172f  /home/user/archives/run_01_artifacts.tar.gz
     41edece42d5da0ad3c0a210b1be62525c53ddb460ff2ac1ab7dbc8b0e2b27f0b  /home/user/restore/run_01/metrics.json
     0a1ba3eab29ffc40e04b0e4d4f98a3e3e6b5b4ed5d4681eb6db8e5f3adf447b8  /home/user/restore/run_01/model.bin
     9f64a747e1e7c913527cc5c2dd4f7f3a2e2298d2c1fa0394e4be9c7c14988b3a  /home/user/restore/run_01/logs.txt
   (each line must end with \n and have exactly two spaces before the absolute path)

The automated tests will:
1. Compute sha256sum of the archive and the three extracted files and compare them with the values written in /home/user/artifact_checksums.log.  
2. Confirm the archive contains exactly the three files with no extra prefix directory.  
3. Confirm restored files match the originals bit-for-bit.  
4. Verify correct ordering and formatting of the log file.
